auth.registerFailed: Registration failed. Email might be already in use.
dashboard.title: Mathe-Quiz Dashboard
dashboard.learning_progress_desc: Your saved progress and solved tasks per module.
dashboard.module_history_desc: Stored tasks and answers for the selected module.
dashboard.history_empty_generic: There is no saved history for this module yet.
dashboard.history_chart_caption_generic: Each bar represents one saved attempt in the selected module. Taller bars took longer.
dashboard.history_overview_generic: Recently stored tasks, answers, and statuses for the selected module.
dashboard.algebra_history_desc: The latest persisted algebra steps from the database.
dashboard.history_chart_caption: Each bar represents one saved algebra step. Taller bars took longer.
dashboard.step_overview: Recently stored transformations with their status and rule.
modules.mental-math_desc: Train your mental math skills with arithmetic operations
modules.fractions_desc: Master fractions and percentages with various operations
modules.algebra_desc: Solve linear and quadratic equations step-by-step
training.question: Question
training.form_equivalent: Your answer is equivalent to the correct answer!
training.click_submit_to_confirm: Click Submit to confirm your answer.
training.help_collapsed: Quick guide for this step-by-step task
training.single_step_instruction: Enter exactly the next valid transformation step. Do not jump to the full solution in one move.
training.mental_math_answer_hint: Enter an integer or decimal answer
training.mental_math_tip_body: Use rough intermediate steps in your head first, then enter only the final answer.
training.mental_math_goal_body: Solve several tasks cleanly in a row without losing pace.
training.mental_math_empty_attempts: No attempts saved yet. Your first answers will appear here immediately.
training.mental_math_focus_1: Solve single basic operations with small numbers reliably.
training.mental_math_focus_2: Answer the same basic operations faster and more consistently.
training.mental_math_focus_3: Process two-digit numbers confidently without long written calculation.
training.mental_math_focus_4: Keep the pace high on larger numbers while keeping errors low.
training.mental_math_focus_5: Handle mixed tasks including division under time pressure.
training.mental_math_range_1: 1 to 10 with addition, subtraction, and multiplication
training.mental_math_range_3: 10 to 99 with larger sums and products
training.mental_math_range_4: 10 to 99 with higher pace and concentration
training.fractions_equivalent_answers_de: Reduced or expanded fractions count as correct as long as they are mathematically equivalent.
training.fractions_percent_answers_desc: When the task asks for a percentage, the system accepts 75 and 75% as equivalent input.
training.fractions_number_answers_desc: For percentage-value tasks, enter only the calculated number result.
training.fractions_tip_body: Check first whether you can reduce the fraction. For addition and subtraction, find a common denominator before entering the final result.
training.percent_tip_body: Watch whether the question asks for a percentage rate or a resulting value. Percentages are often easier when you recognize familiar fractions like 1/2, 1/4, or 3/4.
training.fractions_answer_hint: You can enter a reduced fraction or a whole number when the result works out exactly.
training.fractions_answer_hint_fraction: You can enter a reduced fraction or a whole number when the result works out exactly.
training.fractions_answer_hint_percent: Enter the percentage as a number or with a percent sign.
training.fractions_answer_hint_number: Enter only the calculated number value.
training.fractions_recent_attempts_title: Your recent fraction and percent tasks
training.fractions_empty_attempts: No tasks saved yet. Your next attempts will appear here immediately.
training.fractions_focus_1: Read simple fractions, add them, and recognize first percentage forms.
training.fractions_focus_2: Convert fractions more confidently and spot common percentage patterns.
training.fractions_focus_3: Handle unlike denominators and first percentage-value tasks with control.
training.fractions_focus_4: Mix operation types and choose the right strategy for fractions and percentages.
training.fractions_focus_5: Translate flexibly between fractions, percentage values, and percentage rates.
training.fractions_skill_1: read common denominators or recognize simple percentages like 50% and 25%
training.fractions_skill_2: reduce fractions safely and convert them into familiar percentages
training.fractions_skill_3: align denominators or compute a percentage value from a base and rate
training.fractions_skill_4: recognize the task type quickly and switch between fraction and percent strategies
training.fractions_skill_5: combine reciprocal rules, simplification, and percentage reasoning reliably
training.apply_rule_instruction: Apply the indicated rule exactly once to the current equation.
training.one_step_only_description: Multiple transformations at once count as a step that is too large and will be rejected.
training.completion_instruction: The task is complete once the variable is isolated, meaning the equation is in the form x = value.
training.next_task_instruction: After the final correct step, the next task loads automatically.
training.algebra_level_focus_1: ax + b = c with positive coefficients
training.algebra_level_focus_2: ax + b = c with possible negative coefficients
training.algebra_level_focus_3: Combine multiple terms on one side
training.algebra_level_focus_4: Expand one bracket using the distributive law
training.algebra_level_focus_5: Expand brackets and track terms on both sides
errors.training.syntax_error: The equation is invalid. Please check the syntax.
errors.training.rule_violation: The operation was only applied to one side. You must apply it to both sides.
errors.training.incorrect_step: The step is mathematically incorrect. Check your calculation.
errors.training.too_big_step: The step is too big. Please take one step at a time.
